title: Asigarh Fort
tagline: Asigarh Fort — heritage city of Haryana

overview.short: Asigarh Fort, also called Hansi Fort, is located on the eastern bank of Amti lake in Hansi city of Haryana, India, about 135 km from Delhi on NH9.
overview.description: Asigarh Fort, also called Hansi Fort, is located on the eastern bank of Amti lake in Hansi city of Haryana, India, about 135 km from Delhi on NH9. Spread over 30 acres, in its prime days this fort used to be in control of 80 forts in the area around it. The fort is said to be one of the most impregnable forts of ancient India and has been declared a centrally protected monument by ASI in 1937.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Barsi Gate
topPlaces.description: Barsi Gate is one of the five gates and the only surviving gate that surrounded the walled city of Hansi. It is considered as a good example of Sultanate architecture.

topPlaces.name: Hansi
topPlaces.description: Hansi, is a historically important city and municipal council in the Hansi district of the Indian state of Haryana. Earlier Hansi was larger, more prosperous and more important city than Hisar, as evident by several important buildings of archeological importance.

topPlaces.name: Sheikhpura Kothi
topPlaces.description: Sehikhpura Kothi, currently leased and operated as WelcomHeritage Sheikhpura Kothi, is a heritage building. It was built in 1924 by American architect Dinkelberg for the family of Sir Chhaju Ram Lamba the Jat Zamindar and businessman from Alakhpura, and later leased it to WelcomHeritage Hotels for its current operation as a heritage hotel.

topPlaces.name: Barsi
topPlaces.description: For people with the surname, see Barsi (surname). Barsi is a city in Bawani Khera sub-district of Bhiwani district in Haryana, India.

items.note: Barsi Gate is one of the five gates and the only surviving gate that surrounded the walled city of Hansi. It is considered as a good example of Sultanate architecture.

Hansi, is a historically important city and municipal council in the Hansi district of the Indian state of Haryana. Earlier Hansi was larger, more prosperous and more important city than Hisar, as evident by several important buildings of archeological importance.

Sehikhpura Kothi, currently leased and operated as WelcomHeritage Sheikhpura Kothi, is a heritage building. It was built in 1924 by American architect Dinkelberg for the family of Sir Chhaju Ram Lamba the Jat Zamindar and businessman from Alakhpura, and later leased it to WelcomHeritage Hotels for its current operation as a heritage hotel.
For people with the surname, see Barsi (surname). Barsi is a city in Bawani Khera sub-district of Bhiwani district in Haryana, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Asigarh Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Asigarh Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Asigarh Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Indira Gandhi International Airport, Delhi (126 km) and the nearest railway station is New Delhi Railway Station (133 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Asigarh Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Asigarh Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Asigarh Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Asigarh Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Asigarh Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Barsi Gate, Hansi, Sheikhpura Kothi, Barsi.
